questions.question: A client and server are connected through a datagram socket. The client sends a message 'HELLO' to the server. The server processes the message and sends a response 'WELCOME' back to the client. The client then sends another message 'GOODBYE'. Implement a datagram socket program in Python to display the messages received by the client on the console. Make sure to handle the socket error and disconnections.
questions.hint: Use the socket library in Python and handle exceptions for socket errors and disconnections.

questions.question: A client and server are connected through a datagram socket. The server is running on port 8080 and the client is running on port 8081. The client sends a message 'MESSAGE' to the server. Implement a datagram socket program in Python to display the messages received by the server on the console. Make sure to handle the socket error and disconnections.
questions.hint: Use the socket library in Python and handle exceptions for socket errors and disconnections.

questions.question: A client and server are connected through a datagram socket. The client and server have the same IP address (192.168.1.100) and port number (8080). However, the client is running on port 8081 and the server is running on port 8080. Implement a datagram socket program in Python to display the messages received by the client on the console. Make sure to handle the socket error and disconnections.
questions.hint: Use the socket library in Python and handle exceptions for socket errors and disconnections.
